meta.context-ordering-rule: past context CANNOT be reshaped by future context -- the direction of this relationship is critical
meta.context-compression-warning: acceptable context compression from your current context window in no way guarantees context fidelity in future context -- context is always ephemeral -- write durable state to files
meta.role: narrative protocol schema -- governs the structure of evolving narrative records for any entity with multiple parties and a story that changes over time
meta.intent: combat tribal knowledge and human memory decay -- documentation and SOPs are not enough because they strip narrative out -- this schema preserves narrative structure while making it navigable by agents and humans alike
meta.self-contained: this schema is the sole context source for authoring and maintaining narrative records -- consult only this schema for authoring -- if something required is missing, ask the human rather than reaching outside this file
meta.human-reported-issue-governance: bug reports and feature requests are owned by whatever system the project declares
meta.narrative-bias-acknowledgment: humans operate on narratives -- narrative bias may be the strongest bias humans trend toward -- this schema works with that tendency -- preserving narrative in communication records produces a more accurate record than stripping it -- the goal is structured narrative that stays human-readable
meta.output-format: JSON data artifacts -- not human-editable markdown -- records are written and maintained by agents from human input -- direct human editing is discouraged because it is prone to inconsistency and context leakage between record sets -- humans provide input, agents write the record
meta.storage-recommendation: namespace records by entity type for context isolation -- example: /narratives/clients/acme.json, /narratives/vendors/workorders.json, /narratives/projects/my-book.json -- storage location is the consuming system's decision -- isolation between sets is mandatory -- an agent working on a client record must not have a vendor record in its active context unless the relationship between them is explicitly declared
meta.schema-family: AGENTS-schema-narrative.json is a member of the AGENTS-schema-* family -- it produces JSON data artifacts, not markdown governance files -- AGENTS-schema-governance.json governs code and operations -- AGENTS-schema-1project.json governs single-goal session continuity -- AGENTS-schema-gov2gov-migration.json governs migrations from one governance system into another -- narrative records may reference sibling schema artifacts when work materializes from a narrative thread
meta.sibling-isolation: if another AGENTS-schema-*.json file is present in the context window, treat each schema as operationally independent -- shared field names across schemas are structurally similar but operationally independent -- verify each field against its own schema only -- explicit pointer fields (narrative-ref, project-ref) are the only valid cross-schema references
meta.opening-prompt: The workspace you are in, or the files you see, represents a tool or human workflow that has evolving and updating personal, company, and/or client data, and may be interacted with by nearly every stakeholder involved. We need to build a captured narrative around the experience. This schema describes the mechanisms by which you build that narrative. Implementation and view are up to you, but the mechanisms are not optional. This narrative exists to combat tribal knowledge and human memory decay over time, because documentation and SOPs are unreliable, stale, and have never been enough.

entity-type-hints.role: entity types are an open list -- any entity with an evolving story and multiple parties who need to stay aligned on what happened is a valid subject -- the following are common patterns with notes on which optional fields become most relevant
entity-type-hints.client-account: definition-of-fixed and sentiment-and-tension are critical -- these records reveal whether the relationship is healthy
entity-type-hints.vendor-account: technical-limitations, budgetary-limitations, and awaiting-external are critical -- these records reveal dependency risk
entity-type-hints.employee-workflow: open-threads and tribal flags are critical -- these records reveal training gaps and process friction
entity-type-hints.process: narrative-beats and bad-patterns are critical -- these records reveal where internal workflows break down
entity-type-hints.personal-project: record-state-at-review-open-and-close and open-threads are critical -- these records replace the memory of a solo creator working across long time gaps
entity-type-hints.other: describe the entity and its primary parties -- the agent derives the relevant shape from that description

in-session-capture-triggers.role: rules that fire during an active work session to prevent ambient session state -- open lanes, reframes, observed patterns, proposed decisions -- from existing only in response tokens and evaporating at session end or context compression -- these rules are terrain-layer, not harness-enforced, and describe the shape the agent is expected to hold
in-session-capture-triggers.why-this-exists: the narrative schema's update-cadence (monthly, quarterly, transient) is calendar-based and its signal-cues are passive observations -- neither triggers during a productive session -- without these in-session triggers, a session that accumulates multiple proposals, reframes, or observed patterns loses everything that did not reach a named field before the session closed -- the failure mode is specifically the render-to-human step becoming the only materialization step

render-inversion-before-multi-lane-propo.rule: when the agent is about to present more than one lane, proposal, decision, reframe, or open question to the human in a single response, those items must first be materialized into the narrative record's open-threads, known-decisions, or observed-patterns sections as appropriate -- the response then cites the record path and renders only a summary view of the materialized items
render-inversion-before-multi-lane-propo.constraint: the list must never exist only in the response tokens -- items that reach the human without first reaching the record are lost when context compresses or the session ends
render-inversion-before-multi-lane-propo.what-counts-as-multi-lane: two or more of: proposed actions awaiting human decision, reframes of existing concepts, observed patterns not yet recorded, open questions that the human is expected to answer, recommended next steps that branch -- if any combination of these reaches two or more in a single response, the rule fires
render-inversion-before-multi-lane-propo.render-shape-when-rule-fires: cite the record path -- list only the ids of materialized items and a one-line summary each -- the record holds the durable form, the response holds a view of the record

self-inspection-digest-trigger.rule: at any turn, if the agent could list more than one uncaptured open thread, reframe, or observed pattern accumulated in the current session, the next turn must open with a digest pass before new work
self-inspection-digest-trigger.evaluation-method: the agent evaluates this by asking itself: from the current session, how many items exist that belong in the narrative record but have not yet been written? if the answer is more than one, digest before continuing
self-inspection-digest-trigger.what-the-digest-produces: materialization of accumulated items into the narrative record's appropriate sections -- open-threads for unresolved proposals, known-decisions for choices made and confirmed, observed-patterns for recurring behaviors noticed in passing, entries for beats and reframes
self-inspection-digest-trigger.why-self-inspection-not-counting: a numeric cue-count threshold becomes ceremonial the moment agents start gaming the count -- self-inspection uses the agent's own summarization capability as the check and is harder to route around without noticing

enforcement-posture.what-these-rules-are: terrain -- they shape behavior by describing the shape the agent should hold, but they do not have harness-level interrupt power
enforcement-posture.what-these-rules-are-not: walls -- nothing in the schema prevents an agent from rendering a multi-lane list without first materializing it, and nothing in the schema prevents a session from closing with accumulated uncaptured state
enforcement-posture.how-to-promote-to-walls: pair these rules with Claude Code hooks -- or equivalent harness hooks in other deployments -- that run on UserPromptSubmit, Stop, or PostToolUse events and either inject reminders into context or block the action until the rule is satisfied -- the schema describes the shape, the harness is where enforcement lives -- this separation keeps the schema honest about what it can and cannot do
enforcement-posture.ships-with-schema: example hook configuration is shipped alongside this schema in .claude/settings.json at the workspace root -- adopting deployments may copy, adapt, or replace it

cadence.role: recommended update rhythm per record set -- advisory, set per record at authoring time -- consuming system may enforce or adapt

monthly.scope: set-level -- all records in a set reviewed
monthly.mode: iterative -- append new entries, update open threads, flag stale items -- compress reserved for quarterly only

quarterly.mode: full review -- compress-eligible -- all open threads must be assessed -- all bad patterns reviewed for resolution or escalation

transient.scope: single record -- one-off situation or complaint that cannot wait for the monthly or quarterly cycle
transient.mode: append and update only -- record is closed when the situation resolves -- archived on closure, preserved for quarterly review input

register.role: index of all narrative records in this workspace -- without this, records sprawl and go stale silently

id.role: unique identifier for this record -- used for cross-references from sibling schema artifacts

subject.role: name of the entity this narrative tracks

entity-type.role: what kind of entity this is -- see meta.entity-type-hints for common patterns

record-path.role: path to the JSON record file

last-updated.role: ISO 8601 date of last write to this record

owner.role: who is responsible for keeping this record current

record-structure.role: shape of a single narrative record -- every record regardless of entity type shares this structure -- omit fields that are inapplicable, populate every field that is present

header.role: identity and state of this record

subject.role: one-sentence description of the entity this narrative tracks

entity-type.role: what kind of entity this is

primary-parties.role: who is involved -- names, roles, or relationship descriptions -- at least one entry required

owner.role: who is responsible for keeping this record current

record-state-at-review-open-and-close.role: open and close state for the current update session -- agent reads open at session start, writes close at session end -- same pattern as sibling schemas

open.role: state of the narrative at session start -- what is known, what is open, what changed since last session

close.role: state of the narrative at session end -- what was recorded, what remains open, what the next session should front-load

entries.role: ordered log of events, exchanges, and decisions -- append-only except for status updates on existing entries -- entries are preserved through archival

id.role: unique entry identifier within this record -- used for cross-references and superseded-by links

date.role: ISO 8601 date this entry was recorded

type.role: event, decision, thread-open, thread-close, beat, correction

summary.role: what happened -- preserve the human language as-is -- this is the narrative

tribal.role: true when this information exists only in one person's account with no corroborating record -- tribal entries are highest criticality regardless of apparent significance

cast.role: who was involved in this entry -- include only roles relevant to this entry

owner.role: who owns this issue or thread

context-holder.role: who has the most context -- may differ from owner

definition-of-fixed.role: required when this entry involves a resolution or closure -- a resolution is only real if definitions align

fixed-per-client.role: what the client or requester considers resolved -- in their words

fixed-per-tech.role: what the technician or executor considers resolved -- in their words

definitions-match.role: true when both definitions describe the same outcome -- when false, explain in narrative

matches-original-request.role: true when the resolution matches what was originally asked for -- when false, explain in narrative

divergence-narrative.role: required when definitions-match or matches-original-request is false -- describe the gap and how it was handled or left open

constraints.role: what shaped or limited the outcome -- omit if not applicable

technical-limitations.role: what the technology constrained and why

budgetary-limitations.role: what was descoped or deferred due to cost

awaiting-external.role: blocked on a vendor, partner, or third party -- record who and why

sentiment-and-tension.role: the emotional reality of this entry -- a narrative note, captured in human terms -- did the client feel heard, was there friction, did a relationship shift -- medium criticality by default, high when it predicts churn or disengagement

beat.role: required when type is beat -- describe the direction shift -- a decision reversed, a scope expanded, a new stakeholder appeared, a definition changed -- beats are the moments that rewrite everything that follows and are the first thing tribal memory loses

status.role: open, resolved, archived, superseded -- omit if entry type does not have a lifecycle

superseded-by.role: entry id or external reference that replaces this entry -- preserve original entry as-is

open-threads.role: things raised and still awaiting resolution -- the feature request that was promised and forgotten problem -- populated from entries with type thread-open and status open

id.role: matches entry id that opened this thread

owner.role: who is responsible for resolving this thread

auto-close-trigger.role: observable event that lets an agent close the thread without guessing

stale.role: true when no activity past the declared stale window for this record -- stale open threads are a signal-cue

stale-window.role: how long without activity before this thread is considered stale -- overrides record default if set

known-decisions.role: choices that were made and confirmed -- answers the question did they ask for that or did we make that call

decision.role: what was decided -- in plain language

decided-by.role: who made the call -- attribution is mandatory for known-decisions

requested-by.role: who asked for this outcome -- may differ from decided-by

reversed.role: true if this decision was later reversed -- add a beat entry to entries when this happens

project-ref.role: reference to a sibling schema record if this decision spawned concrete work -- e.g. 1project goal id or governance scope

good.role: patterns worth repeating -- what worked in this relationship or context -- omit if nothing notable

description.role: what worked and why it is worth repeating

tribal.role: true if this pattern exists only in undocumented practice

bad.role: known problems -- if something went wrong record it here -- omit only when nothing has gone wrong -- numbers when they apply, narrative when they do not

description.role: what went wrong and what broke as a side effect

resolved.role: true when addressed -- omit if unknown

tribal.role: true if this pattern exists only in undocumented practice

handoff-note.role: what the next person needs to know beyond what the entries already show -- required on any session that closes a thread or transfers ownership -- this is the tribal knowledge capture point

edit-modes.role: not all touches are equal -- agents must declare which mode they are operating in before writing

append.description: new event, new thread, new decision -- additive only -- nothing prior changes
append.blocked: existing entries stay as-is in append mode

update.description: existing entry changes state -- a thread closes, a decision reverses, a pattern resolves -- old state is preserved as-is alongside the update
update.preservation-rule: add a new entry referencing the updated entry id -- original entry stays as-is

compress.description: multiple entries consolidate into a summary -- highest-risk mode -- governed by compression rules
compress.permitted-when: quarterly review only -- monthly and transient cycles use append and update exclusively
compress.lossy-artifact-warning: compressed entries are lossy artifacts -- detail that lived only in entries and was never promoted to a durable section (known-decisions, open-threads, observed-patterns, handoff-note) during authoring is lost after compression -- this is the accepted cost of compression and the incentive to author well -- treat compressed summaries as directional only, consult durable sections for detail

may-compress: status update entries with no decision, thread, or beat
duplicate sentiment entries where pattern is already captured in observed-patterns
compression-rules.invalid-compress: a compress that loses any never-drop item is invalid -- agent must abort and report which items would be lost

correct.description: factual error fixed -- attribution required -- old value preserved alongside correction
correct.preservation-rule: original entry is preserved as-is -- correction is appended as a new entry with type correction referencing the original entry id

criticality.role: what makes an entry load-bearing vs background noise -- agents use this to prioritize what to front-load in a session and what must survive a compress
criticality.highest: any entry with tribal: true -- regardless of apparent significance
criticality.high: named decisions with named owners -- definition-of-fixed divergences -- narrative beats -- handoff notes
criticality.medium: sentiment-and-tension entries -- open threads with no stale flag -- bad patterns marked resolved
criticality.low: status updates with no decision, thread, or beat -- good patterns with no tribal flag
criticality.escalation-rule: medium criticality escalates to high when sentiment-and-tension predicts churn or disengagement -- agent must flag this to human

signal-cues.role: canonical classes of signal that indicate something worth recording is happening -- used by capture-workflows to detect narrative-relevant events passively
signal-cues.false-positive-tracking: signal cues will misfire -- distinguishing casual language from meaningful signal is approximate -- when an agent flags a cue and the human determines it was noise, record the false positive as a working/pattern entry in the review session -- when a specific cue class consistently misfires in a specific workflow, note it in that workflow's observed patterns and reduce confidence -- keep cues active but adjust trust

context-truncation.description: system shows a fragment but the full story exists upstream
examples: truncated email body in a ticket
ticket note that references an attachment not included
summary field that says see attached with no attachment present
context-truncation.action: flag as incomplete context -- record what is visible -- note that upstream source was captured elsewhere -- treat the fragment as partial, seek the full source

cross-domain-reference.description: a record points outside itself to context it does not contain
examples: note says per our conversation with no record of the conversation
cross-domain-reference.action: flag the reference -- record the fragment -- mark the entry as potentially incomplete -- request the source if accessible

attribution-gap.description: a decision or action is recorded with no named owner
attribution-gap.action: flag the gap -- attempt to infer owner from context -- if unresolvable, record as unknown-owner and mark tribal: true

implicit-history.description: language that signals undocumented prior context
implicit-history.action: flag the reference -- search existing entries for the prior event -- if not found, open a thread to capture what the reference points to

ownership-ambiguity.description: a commitment or task with no named owner
ownership-ambiguity.action: flag as unowned -- record the commitment -- open a thread -- mark stale window from date of entry

stale-open-thread.description: an open thread with no activity past the declared stale window
stale-open-thread.action: flag as stale -- surface in next session open -- escalate to human if no activity in two consecutive cycles

definition-divergence.description: language suggesting the parties are not using the same success criteria
examples: this was fixed in the last update -- with no confirmation from the other party
definition-divergence.action: flag as potential divergence -- record both definitions if available -- populate definition-of-fixed block -- mark resolved only when both parties confirm

tribal-flag.description: information that exists only in one person's account with no corroborating record
examples: only Mike knows why this was set up this way
I remember we decided this but I can't find where
tribal-flag.action: mark tribal: true -- record everything available -- flag as highest criticality -- surface in handoff-note

capture-workflows.role: named workflow patterns describing where narrative-relevant signals appear and what cues to watch for -- an open list, add workflows as patterns are discovered

technician-mailbox.description: inbound communications to a technician or support role -- high signal density for context truncation and cross-domain references
technician-mailbox.notes: email previews and ticket digests are especially prone to context-truncation -- the full thread is the source of truth, not the preview

client-email-thread.description: email exchanges with a client -- high signal density for definition divergence and implicit history
client-email-thread.notes: client language around resolution is rarely identical to technical language -- always check for definition-of-fixed divergence before marking an issue closed

ticket-update.description: updates to a support or project ticket -- moderate signal density, high risk of attribution gaps
ticket-update.notes: ticket status changes without narrative explanation are a common source of tribal knowledge -- record the why, not just the what

vendor-exchange.description: communications with a vendor or external dependency -- high signal density for technical limitations and awaiting-external
vendor-exchange.notes: vendor commitments without named owners and dates are high-risk -- record who said what and when -- verbal commitments are tribal until written

internal-handoff.description: transfer of ownership or context between team members -- highest risk for tribal knowledge loss
internal-handoff.notes: handoff-note is mandatory on any entry that transfers ownership -- a handoff without a handoff-note is an incomplete record

review-session.description: monthly or quarterly review of a record set -- compress-eligible sessions only at quarterly
review-session.notes: review sessions must check every open thread for stale status before closing -- compress is reserved for quarterly review only

chat-history-archival-capture.description: archival extraction of repo-specific decisions, direction, findings, and open threads from current or pasted chat history into narrative arc and vision surfaces
chat-history-archival-capture.timestamp-provenance: decision dates must reflect when the decision was made in the chat, not when capture occurs -- use visible message timestamps first, then session headers or dated transcript markers, then clearly labeled closest-verifiable dates -- never fabricate precise timestamps from transcript order, repo logs, commits, file modification times, or capture time
chat-history-archival-capture.audit-before-write: when the human asks for archival capture or timestamp audit, present proposed entries with destination file, entry type, proposed decision date, timestamp evidence, summary, and uncertainty note before editing
chat-history-archival-capture.capture-boundary: record durable decisions and direction, not every message -- keep repo-specific decisions in that repo and route Anarchy tool/runtime findings to Anarchy source surfaces unless the target repo operating rule changes
chat-history-archival-capture.plugin-companion: the portable schema carries this heuristic so underlay repos can inherit timestamp discipline -- the plugin skill remains the richer execution lane for preflight, JSON validation, cache/source provenance checks, and final state capture

authoring-sequence.role: ordered steps for creating and maintaining narrative records -- two modes: cold-start for new records, update for existing
authoring-sequence.plan-directives: the prompt requires a plan -- the prompt requires plan completion -- make a plan before writing, complete the plan before stopping
cold-start: read this schema fully before writing anything
ask human to describe the entity and its primary parties in plain language
ask human to identify any existing fragments -- emails, tickets, notes, memory -- that contain context about this entity
ask human to declare the cadence: monthly, quarterly, or transient
scan available fragments for signal cues before writing the first entry
author the header block -- report-to-human before writing subject and primary-parties
reconstruct known-decisions from fragments -- flag attribution gaps where owner cannot be determined
reconstruct open-threads from fragments -- mark stale where applicable
record observed-patterns from fragments if any are identifiable
write handoff-note summarizing what was reconstructed and what remains unrecoverable from available fragments
register the record in the register file
declare cold-start complete -- first authoring produces only append entries, compress is reserved for later quarterly review
update: read the existing record header and record-state-at-review-open-and-close.close from the last session before writing anything
declare edit mode before writing: append, update, compress, or correct
scan new input for signal cues before recording
append or update entries according to declared edit mode
update open-threads status for any threads that have moved
update known-decisions if any decisions were reversed or added
update observed-patterns if new patterns emerged
write handoff-note if any thread was closed or ownership transferred

templates.role: starting-point shapes for new records -- replace placeholder values -- only fields declared in this schema are valid -- output is JSON
